content.text: Periodic Compliance Attestations for GenAI Programs

Enterprise GenAI programmes face a calendar of recurring attestation obligations that spans regulatory certifications, internal audit cycles, model risk reviews, and third-party assessments. Unlike event-driven disclosures, periodic attestations are predictable — which means a missed attestation is always a process failure, not a surprise. The compliance calendar must be owned, tracked, and executed with the same discipline as financial close cycles.

This sub-subtopic maps the full annual attestation calendar for a regulated US enterprise with EU operations, defines the evidence required for each attestation, and provides code infrastructure to track obligation status, auto-generate pre-attestation readiness checks, and produce the sign-off workflow that satisfies auditor and regulatory expectations.

The calendar above represents a composite organisation subject to NYDFS Part 500, PCI DSS, SOC 2, ISO 27001, ISO 42001, EU AI Act, SR 11-7 model risk governance, and SEC annual reporting. Not every organisation will face all of these, but the calendar illustrates how attestation obligations cluster and must be managed as an integrated programme rather than separate workstreams.
Covered entity is in compliance with 23 NYCRR 500 cybersecurity requirements; AI systems in scope are documented
Within 90 days of assessment period end
All PCI DSS requirements are met; scope is accurate; AI components in CDE are documented
Trust Service Criteria controls operated effectively throughout the period; GenAI controls tested
ISMS continues to meet ISO 27001; AI-related controls in scope reviewed
AIMS is effective; AI risk register is current; Annex A controls are operating
All material AI/ML models are inventoried, validated, and operating within approved limits
EU AI Act Post-Market Monitoring Report
High-risk / GPAI system performance is monitored; serious incidents reported; corrective actions documented
AI threat landscape is reassessed; risk register updated; treatment plans reviewed
Material AI risks disclosed accurately; no material misstatements or omissions

NYDFS Part 500.17(b) requires a covered entity's highest-ranking executive (CISO or CEO in most organisations) to certify annually that the cybersecurity programme complies with Part 500. Since the 2023 amendments, covered entities must also certify to the specific technical requirements (MFA, encryption, access control, audit logging) rather than a general compliance statement.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date
from enum import Enum

class CheckStatus(str, Enum):
    PASS    = "pass"
    FAIL    = "fail"
    PARTIAL = "partial"
    UNKNOWN = "unknown"

@dataclass
class CertificationCheck:
    requirement: str
    nydfs_section: str
    status: CheckStatus
    evidence_ref: str    # Link to audit evidence artefact
    notes: str = ""

    @property
    def blocks_certification(self) -> bool:
        return self.status == CheckStatus.FAIL

NYDFS_GENAI_CHECKS: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    ("MFA enforced for all GenAI system administrative access", "500.12"),
    ("LLM API credentials stored in approved secrets manager (not in code)", "500.14"),
    ("Prompt scan middleware is operational and audit trail is intact", "500.06"),
    ("Penetration test completed within 12 months; AI endpoints included in scope", "500.05"),
    ("All GenAI third-party providers listed in vendor inventory with risk tier", "500.11"),
    ("Model registry access is logged; logs retained ≥ 3 years", "500.06"),
    ("Annual employee training completion ≥ 95% including AI security module", "500.14(b)"),
    ("Incident response plan covers AI-specific scenarios", "500.16"),
    ("CISO has reviewed and approved AI risk posture for certification period", "500.04(b)"),
]

def run_nydfs_readiness(evidence_store) -> list[CertificationCheck]:
    """
    Run pre-certification checks by querying the evidence store.
    Returns list of checks with status and evidence references.
    """
    results = []
    for requirement, section in NYDFS_GENAI_CHECKS:
        evidence = evidence_store.latest_evidence_for(requirement)
        if evidence is None:
            status = CheckStatus.UNKNOWN
            ref = "NO EVIDENCE"
        elif evidence.is_current:
            status = CheckStatus.PASS
            ref = evidence.artefact_id
        else:
            status = CheckStatus.PARTIAL
            ref = f"{evidence.artefact_id} (STALE: {evidence.date})"
        results.append(CertificationCheck(
            requirement=requirement,
            nydfs_section=section,
            status=status,
            evidence_ref=ref,
        ))
    return results

def certification_is_ready(checks: list[CertificationCheck]) -> bool:
    return not any(c.blocks_certification for c in checks)
SOC 2 Type II — GenAI Control Population

SOC 2 Type II requires that controls not only exist (design effectiveness) but operated consistently throughout the audit period (operating effectiveness). For GenAI, this means the controls defined in the Trust Services Criteria must have run continuously — not just at audit time. Common failures include:

content: The scan middleware was disabled for 3 weeks during a model upgrade and not re-enabled before users resumed sending prompts.
Quarterly access reviews for the model registry were completed in Q1 and Q3 but not Q2 — leaving a gap in the operating-effectiveness period.
CloudWatch log group retention was changed, causing 6 weeks of inference logs to be deleted before the audit period ended.

content.text:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date
from enum import Enum

class ControlFrequency(str, Enum):
    CONTINUOUS = "continuous"   # Must operate every day
    DAILY      = "daily"
    WEEKLY     = "weekly"
    MONTHLY    = "monthly"
    QUARTERLY  = "quarterly"
    ANNUAL     = "annual"

@dataclass
class SOC2Control:
    control_id: str
    tsc_criteria: str     # e.g. "CC6.1", "CC7.2"
    description: str
    frequency: ControlFrequency
    evidence_type: str    # "system_generated", "screenshot", "policy_doc", "report"
    ai_specific: bool
    last_tested: date | None = None
    operating_gaps: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # Date ranges where control lapsed

    @property
    def has_gaps(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.operating_gaps) > 0

SOC2_GENAI_CONTROLS: list[SOC2Control] = [
    SOC2Control("AI-CC6.1-01", "CC6.1", "MFA enforced on all GenAI management consoles",
        ControlFrequency.CONTINUOUS, "system_generated", ai_specific=True),
    SOC2Control("AI-CC6.2-01", "CC6.2", "Prompt scan middleware active and blocking PHI/PAN",
        ControlFrequency.CONTINUOUS, "system_generated", ai_specific=True),
    SOC2Control("AI-CC6.7-01", "CC6.7", "Model registry access reviewed quarterly",
        ControlFrequency.QUARTERLY, "report", ai_specific=True),
    SOC2Control("AI-CC7.2-01", "CC7.2", "LLM inference anomaly alerts reviewed within 24 hours",
        ControlFrequency.DAILY, "system_generated", ai_specific=True),
    SOC2Control("AI-CC9.2-01", "CC9.2", "LLM vendor (BA/subprocessor) assessed annually",
        ControlFrequency.ANNUAL, "report", ai_specific=True),
    SOC2Control("AI-A1.2-01", "A1.2", "LLM API availability monitored; SLA breaches tracked",
        ControlFrequency.CONTINUOUS, "system_generated", ai_specific=True),
    SOC2Control("AI-PI1.5-01", "PI1.5", "Model output accuracy sampled weekly against ground truth",
        ControlFrequency.WEEKLY, "report", ai_specific=True),
]

def population_for_period(controls: list[SOC2Control], start: date, end: date) -> dict:
    """
    For each control, estimate the number of expected evidence samples
    in the audit period based on frequency.
    """
    from math import ceil
    days = (end - start).days
    freq_samples = {
        ControlFrequency.CONTINUOUS: days,
        ControlFrequency.DAILY: days,
        ControlFrequency.WEEKLY: ceil(days / 7),
        ControlFrequency.MONTHLY: ceil(days / 30),
        ControlFrequency.QUARTERLY: ceil(days / 91),
        ControlFrequency.ANNUAL: 1,
    }
    return {c.control_id: freq_samples[c.frequency] for c in controls}

The Federal Reserve's SR 11-7 guidance requires periodic validation of all material models. For GenAI, an LLM used in a credit decision, fraud detection, or regulatory capital calculation is a material model. The annual review must cover:

content: Does the model still do what it was designed to do? Has the task definition or data distribution shifted since initial validation?
Has the model performed within approved accuracy and bias thresholds throughout the year? Are there documented performance degradation events?
Are all LLMs in use registered in the model inventory? Inventory gaps — shadow AI models deployed without governance approval — are a leading finding in examinations.
Is the model being used only for its approved purpose? A fraud detection LLM being repurposed for customer communications without re-validation is a governance failure.

content.text:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date
from enum import Enum

class ModelTier(str, Enum):
    MATERIAL  = "material"     # Full annual validation required
    IMMATERIAL = "immaterial"  # Simplified review
    SHADOW    = "shadow"       # Discovered but not yet governed — escalate immediately

class ValidationStatus(str, Enum):
    CURRENT     = "current"     # Validated within required period
    DUE         = "due"         # Within 30-day warning window
    OVERDUE     = "overdue"     # Past validation due date
    NEVER       = "never"       # No validation on record

@dataclass
class ModelInventoryEntry:
    model_id: str
    name: str
    tier: ModelTier
    use_case: str
    approved_use: str
    owner: str
    deployment_date: date
    last_validation_date: date | None
    next_validation_due: date | None
    validation_status: ValidationStatus = ValidationStatus.NEVER

    def compute_status(self, as_of: date | None = None) -> ValidationStatus:
        today = as_of or date.today()
        if self.next_validation_due is None or self.last_validation_date is None:
            return ValidationStatus.NEVER
        days_remaining = (self.next_validation_due - today).days
        if days_remaining < 0:
            return ValidationStatus.OVERDUE
        if days_remaining <= 30:
            return ValidationStatus.DUE
        return ValidationStatus.CURRENT

class ModelRiskAnnualReview:
    def __init__(self, inventory: list[ModelInventoryEntry]):
        self._inventory = inventory

    def overdue_models(self) -> list[ModelInventoryEntry]:
        return [m for m in self._inventory
                if m.compute_status() == ValidationStatus.OVERDUE]

    def shadow_models(self) -> list[ModelInventoryEntry]:
        return [m for m in self._inventory if m.tier == ModelTier.SHADOW]

    def certification_ready(self) -> bool:
        """Annual review can be certified only if no material models are overdue.""""
        material_overdue = [
            m for m in self.overdue_models() if m.tier == ModelTier.MATERIAL
        ]
        return len(material_overdue) == 0 and len(self.shadow_models()) == 0

    def summary_report(self) -> dict:
        total = len(self._inventory)
        overdue = len(self.overdue_models())
        shadow = len(self.shadow_models())
        return {
            "total_models": total,
            "material": sum(1 for m in self._inventory if m.tier == ModelTier.MATERIAL),
            "overdue": overdue,
            "shadow_ai_discovered": shadow,
            "certification_ready": self.certification_ready(),
        }

Every periodic attestation requires a documented approval chain. For regulatory certifications, the signer carries personal liability — NYDFS Part 500 certifications are signed by a senior officer under penalty of law. The sign-off workflow must capture who approved the underlying evidence, who conducted the readiness review, and who ultimately signed the certification.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import Enum
import hashlib, uuid

class AttestationRole(str, Enum):
    CONTROL_OWNER   = "control_owner"     # Confirms evidence is accurate
    RISK_STEWARD    = "risk_steward"      # Validates control effectiveness
    LEGAL_COUNSEL   = "legal_counsel"     # Reviews regulatory language
    CISO            = "ciso"              # Signs cybersecurity attestations
    CEO_CFO         = "ceo_cfo"           # Signs SEC filings
    AI_GOV_OFFICER  = "ai_gov_officer"    # Signs EU AI Act / ISO 42001

class ApprovalStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING  = "pending"
    APPROVED = "approved"
    REJECTED = "rejected"

@dataclass
class ApprovalStep:
    step_number: int
    role: AttestationRole
    approver_name: str
    approver_email: str
    status: ApprovalStatus = ApprovalStatus.PENDING
    approved_at: str | None = None
    comments: str = ""

    def approve(self, comments: str = "") -> None:
        if self.status != ApprovalStatus.PENDING:
            raise ValueError(f"Step {self.step_number} is already {self.status}")
        self.status = ApprovalStatus.APPROVED
        self.approved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        self.comments = comments

@dataclass
class AttestationPackage:
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())[:8])
    attestation_type: str = ""     # e.g. "NYDFS_Part500_Annual"
    period_start: str = ""
    period_end: str = ""
    submission_deadline: str = ""
    evidence_bundle_hash: str = ""  # SHA-256 of the zipped evidence package
    approval_chain: list[ApprovalStep] = field(default_factory=list)
    filed_at: str | None = None

    def current_step(self) -> ApprovalStep | None:
        for step in self.approval_chain:
            if step.status == ApprovalStatus.PENDING:
                return step
        return None

    def is_fully_approved(self) -> bool:
        return all(s.status == ApprovalStatus.APPROVED for s in self.approval_chain)

    def record_filing(self, confirmation_ref: str) -> None:
        if not self.is_fully_approved():
            raise ValueError("Cannot file: approval chain not complete")
        self.filed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

def build_nydfs_package(period_year: int) -> AttestationPackage:
    from datetime import date
    return AttestationPackage(
        attestation_type="NYDFS_Part500_Annual",
        period_start=f"{period_year}-01-01",
        period_end=f"{period_year}-12-31",
        submission_deadline=f"{period_year + 1}-02-15",
        approval_chain=[
            ApprovalStep(1, AttestationRole.CONTROL_OWNER, "VP Engineering", "vpe@corp.com"),
            ApprovalStep(2, AttestationRole.RISK_STEWARD, "Director Risk", "drisk@corp.com"),
            ApprovalStep(3, AttestationRole.LEGAL_COUNSEL, "General Counsel", "gc@corp.com"),
            ApprovalStep(4, AttestationRole.CISO, "CISO", "ciso@corp.com"),
        ],
    )

Manually assembling attestation evidence packages under deadline pressure introduces errors and omissions. Automated evidence collection — pulling system-generated artefacts from audit trails, model registries, and access review systems — reduces preparation time and ensures evidence is tamper-evident.

from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date
import hashlib, json

@dataclass
class EvidenceArtefact:
    artefact_id: str
    control_id: str
    source_system: str
    collection_date: date
    content_hash: str       # SHA-256 of the raw evidence content
    file_path: str          # In WORM storage
    attestation_type: str

class EvidenceCollector:
    """
    Pulls evidence from multiple source systems and assembles
    an attestation evidence bundle.
    """
    def __init__(self, evidence_store):
        self._store = evidence_store

    def collect_for_attestation(
        self, attestation_type: str, period_start: date, period_end: date
    ) -> list[EvidenceArtefact]:
        artefacts = []

        # Pull system-generated logs (continuous controls)
        artefacts += self._store.query_logs(
            control_tags=[attestation_type],
            start=period_start, end=period_end,
        )
        # Pull periodic review reports (quarterly, annual)
        artefacts += self._store.query_reports(
            attestation_type=attestation_type,
            start=period_start, end=period_end,
        )
        return artefacts

    def bundle_hash(self, artefacts: list[EvidenceArtefact]) -> str:
        """
        Compute a deterministic hash of the entire evidence bundle.
        Used to bind the evidence set to the attestation signature.
        """"
        manifest = sorted(a.content_hash for a in artefacts)
        return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(manifest).encode()).hexdigest()

    def coverage_gaps(
        self, controls: list["SOC2Control"], artefacts: list[EvidenceArtefact],
        period_start: date, period_end: date,
    ) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
        """
        Identify controls that have insufficient evidence for the period.
        Returns {control_id: [gap description]}.
        """"
        covered = {a.control_id for a in artefacts}
        gaps: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
        for ctrl in controls:
            if ctrl.control_id not in covered:
                gaps[ctrl.control_id] = [f"No evidence found for period {period_start}–{period_end}"]
        return gaps
Common Attestation Failures in GenAI Programmes
Shadow AI — team bypassed governance to move fast
Gate CI/CD pipeline: deployment to production requires model_id in registry
Prompt scan logs deleted before audit period ends
Default log retention too short (7 or 30 days)
Set minimum retention in log group policy; enforce via IaC; alert on retention changes
Access review skipped for one quarter
Manual process with no tracking; owner changed roles
Automate access review scheduling; send escalations 14 days before due date
Vendor BAA expired during audit period
Renewal not tracked; legal team unaware of expiry
BAA expiry dates in contract management system with 90-day renewal alerts
NYDFS certification signed by wrong authority level
Delegated signer was below required officer level
Approval workflow enforces role check before signature step is unlocked
SOC 2 control gap due to infrastructure migration
Controls not re-implemented on new platform before audit
Continuity plan: maintain parallel evidence stream during migrations

Periodic attestations are most efficiently managed as a single integrated programme rather than separate workstreams per regulation. A consolidated compliance calendar, a shared evidence repository, and a unified approval workflow reduce duplication — many controls (access control, logging, encryption, vendor management) are assessed once and mapped to multiple attestation requirements simultaneously.

content: One WORM-backed store for all compliance evidence, tagged by control, attestation type, and period. SOC 2 auditors, NYDFS examiners, and ISO auditors all draw from the same source.

Maintain a control-to-attestation matrix: for each internal control, which attestations does it satisfy? A single prompt-scan monitoring control may satisfy SOC 2 CC7.2, NYDFS 500.06, and ISO 27001 A.12.4 simultaneously.

A real-time view of which attestations are current, which are approaching their deadline, and which have evidence gaps. Reviewed weekly by the compliance team and monthly by the CISO and board audit committee.

content.text: Attestation calendar locked in H2 of prior year:

content: For the following year, all deadlines, owners, and evidence requirements should be confirmed and loaded into the tracking system before December 31. Last-minute scrambles in February cause NYDFS certification errors.

content.text: Periodic compliance attestations for GenAI programmes require the same operational discipline as financial reporting: defined owners, automated evidence collection, documented approval chains, and a consolidated calendar managed well in advance of deadlines. The NYDFS February 15 certification, SOC 2 audit period continuity, SR 11-7 annual model validation, and EU AI Act post-market monitoring reports are predictable obligations — missing them is always a process failure, not a surprise.
The most effective posture is to
operate in a perpetually audit-ready state

: controls run continuously, evidence is collected automatically, and the evidence store is always current. Attestation then becomes an assembly and sign-off exercise rather than an evidence-gathering sprint. GenAI-specific controls (prompt scanning, model registry, output monitoring) must be brought into the same continuous-operation model as traditional IT controls, with the same gap-detection alerting and escalation paths.

The sign-off workflow is the final risk gate before a certification is filed. For regulatory attestations where the signer carries personal liability (NYDFS, SEC), the approval chain must include legal review of the certification language and CISO confirmation that the underlying evidence supports the statement. Automate the workflow so no step can be skipped, and ensure the evidence bundle hash is cryptographically bound to the signed document.
